title: Angelica Ross hosts a US presidential forum as an openly transgender woman
description: Actress and activist Angelica Ross moderated a United States presidential candidate forum, the first openly transgender person to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mattel releases the first gender-neutral doll line
description: Toy maker Mattel put its Creatable World dolls on sale, described as the first line of gender-neutral dolls on the global market.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Swe Zin Htet competes at Miss Universe as an openly lesbian contestant
description: Miss Universe Myanmar, Swe Zin Htet, took part in the Miss Universe pageant as its first openly lesbian contestant. Spain's Patricia Yurena Rodríguez had competed in 2013 but came out only after that contest.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: London holds its first Trans Pride march
description: The first Trans Pride march in London took place, giving the city a dedicated protest and visibility event for trans and non-binary people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Megan Rapinoe appears in the Sports Illustrated swimsuit issue
description: Footballer Megan Rapinoe was featured in the annual Sports Illustrated swimsuit issue, the first openly gay woman to appear in it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andy Brennan comes out as the first openly gay male Australian soccer player
description: Andy Brennan publicly came out as gay, a first among male soccer players in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dutee Chand speaks publicly about her same-sex relationship
description: Sprinter Dutee Chand said publicly that she is in a same-sex relationship, the first Indian athlete to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nyla Rose signs with All Elite Wrestling
description: Wrestler Nyla Rose joined All Elite Wrestling, the first openly transgender woman under contract with a major American professional wrestling promotion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: An openly gay couple plays doubles together at Wimbledon
description: Belgian players Alison van Uytvanck and Greet Minnen entered the Wimbledon doubles draw as partners on and off the court, the first openly gay couple to team up at the tournament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: June Eastwood runs NCAA Division I cross country as a transgender woman
description: June Eastwood competed for the University of Montana women's team, the first openly transgender woman in NCAA Division I cross country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amanda Sauer-Cook officiates in a major professional football league
description: Amanda Sauer-Cook worked as a center judge in the Alliance of American Football, the first openly gay referee in a major professional football league.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Northern Ireland
description: Marriage between same-sex partners became legal in Northern Ireland, the last part of the United Kingdom where it had remained unavailable.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Costa Rica
description: Costa Rica's marriage law began covering same-sex couples, making it the first country in Central America to allow same-sex marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sark passes same-sex marriage into effect
description: The Channel Island of Sark, a British Crown dependency, both passed and brought into force legislation allowing same-sex couples to marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tlaxcala legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The Mexican state of Tlaxcala passed and brought into force a law opening marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nevada writes same-sex marriage protection into its constitution
description: Voters in Nevada approved a ballot measure anchoring the right to same-sex marriage in the state constitution, the first US state to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil union law takes effect in Monaco
description: Monaco's cohabitation-contract legislation came into force, giving same-sex couples a form of registered partnership.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil partnership law takes effect in the Cayman Islands
description: Civil partnership legislation came into force in the Cayman Islands, a British Overseas Territory, giving same-sex couples legal recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Montenegro passes a civil union law
description: Montenegro's parliament approved legislation on life partnerships for same-sex couples, the first Balkan state outside the EU to adopt such a law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gabon decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Gabon repealed the 2019 provision criminalizing same-sex sexual relations, removing the offense from its penal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sudan ends the death penalty and flogging for homosexuality
description: Sudan removed capital punishment and flogging from the penalties for same-sex acts. Homosexuality remained criminalized, but with prison terms instead of execution.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Supreme Court bars employment discrimination against LGBTQ workers
description: In Bostock v. Clayton County the US Supreme Court ruled that firing someone for being gay or transgender violates Title VII of the Civil Rights Act, extending federal employment protection nationwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Switzerland extends anti-discrimination law to sexual orientation
description: Swiss voters approved a referendum adding sexual orientation to the criminal-law ban on discrimination and incitement to hatred.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Barbados adopts anti-discrimination protections
description: Barbados put in place legal protections against discrimination covering sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: North Macedonia adopts anti-discrimination protections
description: North Macedonia enacted anti-discrimination legislation covering sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marshall Islands adopts anti-discrimination protections
description: The Marshall Islands introduced legal protections against discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pete Buttigieg wins a US presidential nominating contest
description: Pete Buttigieg took the Iowa caucuses in the Democratic race, the first openly gay candidate to win an American presidential primary or caucus.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Richard Grenell serves at US cabinet level as an openly gay official
description: Richard Grenell held the post of acting director of national intelligence in the Trump administration for several months, the first openly gay person in a US cabinet-level position.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First out transgender woman elected to a political position in Louisiana
description: Peyton Rose Michelle Theriot won the 46th District seat A on the women's Democratic State Central Committee, representing St. Martin, Iberia and St. Landry parishes — the first out transgender woman elected to any political position in Louisiana.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Karine Jean-Pierre named vice presidential chief of staff
description: Karine Jean-Pierre took on the role of chief of staff to a US vice presidential candidate, the first openly gay woman in that position.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stormie Forte joins the Raleigh City Council
description: Stormie Forte became the first openly LGBTQ woman to sit on the city council of Raleigh, North Carolina.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Openly gay speakers take a keynote slot at the Democratic National Convention
description: Malcolm Kenyatta, Sam Park and Robert Garcia delivered a keynote at the Democratic National Convention, the first openly gay speakers in that slot.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sarah McBride elected as the first transgender state senator in the US
description: Sarah McBride won a seat in the Delaware State Senate, the first transgender person elected to a state senate anywhere in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay Black men elected to the US Congress
description: Mondaire Jones and Ritchie Torres won House seats in New York, the first openly gay Black men in Congress; Torres was also the first openly gay Afro-Latino member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taylor Small elected to the Vermont General Assembly
description: Taylor Small won election to Vermont's legislature, the first transgender person to serve in the Vermont General Assembly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mauree Turner elected as the first non-binary US state legislator
description: Mauree Turner won a seat in the Oklahoma House of Representatives, the first openly non-binary person elected to any state legislature in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stephanie Byers elected to the Kansas House of Representatives
description: Stephanie Byers, a member of the Chickasaw Nation, won a Kansas House seat — the first Native American transgender person elected to public office in the US and the first transgender member of the Kansas legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Joe Biden mentions transgender people in his victory speech
description: President-elect Joe Biden named the transgender community in his election victory speech, the first US president-elect to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Christy Holstege becomes the first openly bisexual US mayor
description: Christy Holstege took office as mayor of Palm Springs, California, the first openly bisexual mayor in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alex Lee joins the California State Assembly
description: Alex Lee was elected to the California State Assembly, its first openly bisexual member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ryan Fecteau elected Speaker of the Maine House
description: Ryan Fecteau took over as Speaker of the Maine House of Representatives, the first openly gay person in that office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sean Patrick Maloney elected DCCC chair
description: Sean Patrick Maloney won the chairmanship of the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, the first openly gay person elected to lead it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Martin Jenkins sworn in to the California Supreme Court
description: Martin Jenkins joined the California Supreme Court, its first openly gay justice.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Todd Gloria elected mayor of San Diego
description: Todd Gloria won the San Diego mayoral race, the city's first openly gay mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pete Buttigieg nominated as the first openly gay US cabinet nominee
description: President-elect Joe Biden selected Pete Buttigieg as his nominee for Secretary of Transportation, the first openly gay cabinet nominee in US history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David Ortiz elected to the Colorado legislature
description: David Ortiz won a seat in the Colorado House of Representatives, the first openly bisexual person elected as a legislator in the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Layla Moran comes out as pansexual
description: Member of Parliament Layla Moran said in an interview that she is pansexual, believed to be the first UK parliamentarian to come out as such.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Petra De Sutter becomes Europe's first transgender deputy prime minister
description: Petra De Sutter was sworn in as one of seven deputy prime ministers in the Belgian government of Alexander De Croo, making her the most senior transgender politician in Europe.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Levi Davis comes out as bisexual while playing professional rugby union
description: Rugby union player Levi Davis came out as bisexual, the first professional in the sport to do so during his playing career.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Katie Sowers coaches in a Super Bowl
description: Katie Sowers worked the Super Bowl as an offensive assistant, the first woman and first openly gay coach to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Curdin Orlik comes out as the first openly gay active Swiss pro athlete
description: Wrestler Curdin Orlik came out as gay, the first athlete in the Swiss sport of Schwingen to do so and the first openly gay man active in Swiss professional sports.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sebastian Vega comes out as Argentina's first openly gay pro basketball player
description: Basketball player Sebastian Vega publicly came out as gay, a first among professional players in Argentina.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Mosier competes in a US Olympic trial
description: Chris Mosier raced in an Olympic trial alongside other men, the first openly transgender male athlete to do so, though injury kept him from finishing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Megan Youngren runs the US Olympic marathon trials
description: Megan Youngren competed at the US Olympic marathon trials, the first openly transgender athlete in that event's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Camille Balanche wins the downhill mountain-bike world title
description: Swiss rider Camille Balanche took the UCI Downhill Mountain Biking World Championship, the first out lesbian to win the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mara Gómez plays top-flight football in Argentina as a trans woman
description: Footballer Mara Gómez debuted in Argentina's top women's league, the first trans player at that level of the country's game.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Soleil 'Ewok' Wheeler comes out as transgender
description: FaZe Clan streamer Soleil 'Ewok' Wheeler came out as a transgender man, the first openly transgender male player on a tier-one esports organization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
